between_subjects_variables.name: anchor_condition
between_subjects_variables.description: High vs. Low anchor provided before estimation.

participants.description: Students at University of California, Berkeley

meat.text: Amount of meat eaten per year by average American (in pounds)

sf_nyc.text: Distance from San Francisco to New York City (in miles)

female_profs.text: Number of female professors at the University of California, Berkeley

babies.text: Average number of babies born per day in the United States

cat_speed.text: Maximum speed of house cat (in miles per hour)

gas_usage.text: Amount of gas used per month by average American (in gallons)

bars_berkeley.text: Number of bars in Berkeley, CA

colleges_ca.text: Number of state colleges and universities in California

instructions.general: You will be asked to estimate a quantity. Before estimating, you will be shown a randomly generated number.
instructions.task: First, indicate whether the true value is higher or lower than the number. Then, provide your best estimate.

procedure.description: Standard anchoring paradigm: Comparative judgment followed by absolute estimation.
